titleStmt.title: SII 36.252: original edition by P Shanmugam
No. 252. (A.R. No. 252 of 1910.) IḌAIYARPĀKKAM (Eḍayarpākkam), SRIPERUMBUDUR TALUK, CHINGELPUT DISTRICT. On the north base of the Śiva temple.

summary.p: Gift of 95 sheep by Vēlaṉ Madhurāntakaṉ from Kōṉēri in Kōṉērināḍu, a subdivision of Eyiṟ-kōṭṭam, in Jayaṅkoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam to the temple of Tiruppaḍakkāḍuḍaiya Mahādēva at Purisai in Purisai-nāḍu, a subdivision of Maṇaviṟ-kōṭṭam. The sheep were received by the local shepherd, Paṅkiyañchi. He agreed to provide one uḻakku of ghee in the temple treasury for the maintenance of one lamp. Mentions that the Chandrasēkharaṉ Ravi alias Chōḻēndrasiṅga Ācāriyaṉ constructed the stone temple.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī pukaḻmātu viḷaṅkac cayamātu virumpa nilamakaṇilava malarmakaḷ puṇara Urimaiyiṟ ciṟan=ta maṇimuṭi cūṭi mīnavar nilaikeṭa villavar kulaitara Enaimannavaririyaluṟṟaḻitarat tikkanaittun=tan cakkara
naṭātti vīrasimhāsanattu Avanimuḻutuṭaiyāḷoṭum vīṟṟirun=taruḷiya korājakesariparmmarāna cakravattikaḷ śrī kulottuṅgacoḷadevaṟku yāṇṭu 38 Āvatu jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻa maṇṭalattu maṇaviṟ
koṭṭattup puricai nāṭṭup puricait tiruppaṭakkāṭuṭaiya mahādevaṟku Immaṇṭalattu Eyiṟ koṭṭattuk koneri nāṭṭuk koneriyil Aracūr kiḻavan veḷān maturān=takanen vaitta tirunan=tāviḷakku Oṉṟukku
viṭṭa Āṭu 9 10 5 Ivvāṭu toṇṇūṟṟaiñcum cāvāmūvāp perāṭāka Ittevar curapi Iṭaiyan paṅkiyañciyen kaikkoṇṭu Ittevar paṇṭāratte nicatam Uḻakku ney nānum En vaṟkattārum A
ḷakka kaṭavomākavumitu muṭṭil muṭṭiraṭṭi neyyaḷakkak kaṭavenākac cammatittāṭu toṇṇūṟṟaiñcuṅ kaikkoṇṭu can=tirātittar Uḷḷaḷavum Aḷakkak kaṭavenānen paṅkiyañciyen Itu cilālekai
ceyvitten perumāḷ tiruvāymoḻin=taruḷa Ittiruk kaṟṟaḷi Eṭuppitta can=tiracekaran Iraviyāna coḷentraciṅka Ācāriyanen Ivai En Eḻuttu Ittanmattaic celuttāte virota ninṟār
kaṅkaiyiṭai kumariyiṭaiceytār ceyta pāvattup paṭuvār Itu panmāheśvara rakṣai śrī
Digital edition of SII 36.252 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
